overview: The JSON Filter processes JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) files for translation. It extracts string values — both standalone and key-associated — as translatable content, with flexible control over which paths to extract using regex rules. The filter supports multiple Unicode encodings with BOM detection, inline code patterns for placeholders, sub-filtering for embedded content (e.g., HTML within JSON values), and metadata extraction rules for notes, IDs, and length constraints.

extraction.extractAll.help:
Controls the default extraction behavior for key/value pairs.

- **true**: All key/value string pairs are extracted, except those matching `excludeKeys`.
- **false**: No key/value pairs are extracted unless they match `pathRules`.

Use `excludeKeys` or `pathRules` to fine-tune which content is included or excluded.

dependsOn.condition: pathRules overrides this setting when specified

extraction.pathRules.help:
Regex matching full JSON key paths of values to extract. **Overrides `extractAll` when specified.** Paths use `/`-separated key names.

This is an M39+ extraction rule that takes precedence over the older extractAll + excludeKeys approach.
extraction.pathRules.values: Java regex applied against the full key path (e.g. `/menu/popup/menuitem/value`). Use `.*` wildcards for subtrees.

extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.help: Extracts string values that appear in arrays without an associated key. For example, in `["Hello", "World"]`, the strings have no key — they are standalone/isolated. Without this option, only key-value pairs like `{"greeting": "Hello"}` are extracted.

extraction.excludeKeys.help:
Keys whose string values should be excluded from extraction (when `extractAll` is true) or included (when `extractAll` is false — inverted behavior).

When `useFullPath` is enabled in the keys settings, the regex is matched against the full path (e.g. `/root/excludedStructure/key`).
extraction.excludeKeys.values: Comma-separated key names or a regex pattern. With `useFullKeyPath`, use path patterns like `^.*?/excludedStructure/.*`.
examples: `^.*?/excludedStructure/.*` — exclude an entire nested structure (requires full key path)

dependsOn.condition: when enabled, regex patterns apply to full paths instead of key names

extraction.subfilterRules.help:
Specify an Okapi filter ID to process the content of all translatable text values with a sub-filter. For example, use `okf_html` to process HTML markup embedded within JSON string values.

Leave blank for default behavior (no sub-filtering).
notes: **Cannot be used together with inline codes.** If sub-filtering is enabled, the inline code finder is ignored.
examples: `okf_html` — process HTML tags within JSON string values

keys.useAsName.help: Uses the JSON key as the `resname` (resource name / Block name) for the extracted text unit. This makes it easier to identify translations by their original JSON key in downstream tools.

keys.useFullPath.help:
Uses the complete path from root to the key (e.g. `/menu/popup/menuitem/value`) instead of just the immediate key name. `useAsName` must be enabled for this to take effect.

When enabled, exception regex patterns in `excludeKeys` also match against the full path.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for useFullPath to take effect

keys.leadingSlash.help:
Controls whether the full key path starts with a `/` character. Only applies when `useFullPath` is enabled.

- **true**: `/menu/popup/value`
- **false**: `menu/popup/value`

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when useFullPath is enabled

keys.useIdStack.help: Builds text unit IDs by stacking nested JSON keys. Useful for creating deterministic, path-based IDs for deeply nested structures.

keys.idRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **values** become the `resname` (Block ID) of sibling text units. This overrides `useAsName`.

Useful when the JSON structure stores IDs as values in a sibling key rather than using the key name itself.
keys.idRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
examples: Given `{"key": "datePicker_march", "text": "March"}`, setting idRules to `key` produces a text unit with `resname="datePicker_march"` for the "March" value.

metadata.noteRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **values** are attached as translator notes (`<note>` in XLIFF) to sibling text units.

Useful for including descriptions or comments that help translators understand context.
metadata.noteRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
examples: `/widgets/name.*` — all name-like keys under widgets become notes

metadata.metaRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **values** are added as generic metadata (`<context-group>` in XLIFF) to sibling text units.

This metadata is preserved through the translation pipeline and can be used by downstream tools.
metadata.metaRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
examples: `/widgets/image.*` — attach image-related metadata to text units

metadata.maxwidthRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose numeric **values** define maximum width constraints for sibling text units. The matched value becomes the `maxwidth` attribute in XLIFF.

- The matched value applies to **all other elements at the same array level**.
- Only **one** matching key is allowed per hierarchy level.
- For nested arrays, each level can have its own maxwidth definition.
- If no key matches at a sublevel but a parent level has a match, the parent's value applies to the deepest level only.
- When both parent and child levels have matches, the parent's matching key must appear **after** the last child element.
metadata.maxwidthRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
notes: Only one matching array element per hierarchy level is allowed.
For parent-level keys to apply correctly with child-level keys, the parent matching key must be defined after the last child element.

maxwidthSizeUnit.help:
The string value used as the `size-unit` attribute on XLIFF `<trans-unit>` elements when `maxwidthRules` extracts length restrictions.

Common values include `char`, `byte`, `pixel`, etc.
maxwidthSizeUnit.values: Free-form string used as the XLIFF `size-unit` attribute value (e.g. `char`, `byte`, `pixel`).

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when maxwidthRules is configured

output.escapeSlashes.help:
Controls whether forward slashes (`/`) are escaped as `\/` in the output JSON. JSON allows both forms, but some systems expect escaped slashes.

- **true** (default): forward slashes are escaped (`\/`)
- **false**: forward slashes are written as-is (`/`)

inlineCodes.enabled.help:
Enables pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, format specifiers, escape sequences, etc.) within extracted text. Matched patterns are converted to inline code placeholders that translators cannot accidentally modify.

The default pattern set detects printf-style format specifiers (`%s`, `%d`), escape sequences (`\n`, `\t`), and ICU-style placeholders (`{0}`).
notes: **Cannot be used together with the sub-filtering option.** If a sub-filter is configured, inline code detection is ignored.

inlineCodes.rules.help:
Regex patterns that identify inline codes within translatable text. Each pattern match is converted to an inline code placeholder.

The default patterns detect:
- Printf-style specifiers: `%s`, `%d`, `%2$s`
- Escape sequences: `\n`, `\r`, `\t`
- ICU/positional placeholders: `{0}`, `{name}`
inlineCodes.rules.values: Each rule contains a `pattern` field with a Java regex. Use the sample field and presets to test patterns.
examples: `<[^>]+>` — detect HTML tags as inline codes

processingNotes: JSON files are normally in Unicode encoding, but the filter supports any encoding. Encoding is determined by: BOM detection first, then `charset` declaration in the first 1000 characters, then default encoding.
If output encoding is UTF-8 and input was also UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input. If input was not UTF-8, no BOM is written.
Line-break style (CRLF/LF) of the output matches the input document.
Though not technically legal in JSON, the filter supports comments: `// comment`, `# comment`, `/* comment */`, and `<!-- comment -->`.
Comments within a JSON string value are parsed as part of the string content, not as actual comments.
limitations: Comments within a JSON string are parsed as part of the string content, not as comments. A configured sub-filter will then process these as true comments.
Inline codes and sub-filtering cannot be used simultaneously — enabling sub-filtering disables inline code detection.

examples.title: Basic JSON file extraction
examples.description: A simple JSON file with key-value pairs. All string values associated with keys are extracted by default.

examples.title: Using extraction rules with ID rules
examples.description: Extract specific paths and use sibling key values as resource names. Given JSON with `key` and `text` pairs, the `idRules` regex `key` causes the value of the `key` field to become the `resname` of the `text` field's text unit.

examples.title: FPRM-style extraction rules (M39+)
examples.description: The M39+ extraction rules override the older extractAll + exceptions approach. These regex patterns match against full key paths.
examples.config:
```yaml
extraction:
  pathRules: "/widgets/body.*"
metadata:
  noteRules: "/widgets/name.*"
  metaRules: "/widgets/image.*"
keys:
  idRules: "/widgets/id.*"
```

maxwidthSizeUnit.title: Max Width Size Unit
maxwidthSizeUnit.description: The size-unit attribute value used for XLIFF length restriction elements.

fullDoc:
# JSON Filter

The JSON Filter processes JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) files for translation. It extracts string values — both standalone and key-associated — as translatable content, with flexible control over which paths to extract using regex rules. The filter supports multiple Unicode encodings with BOM detection, inline code patterns for placeholders, sub-filtering for embedded content (e.g., HTML within JSON values), and metadata extraction rules for notes, IDs, and length constraints.

## Parameters

### Extraction

#### Extract All
Controls the default extraction behavior for key/value pairs. When **true**, all key/value string pairs are extracted, except those matching `excludeKeys`. When **false**, no key/value pairs are extracted unless they match `pathRules`.

#### Path Rules
Regex matching full JSON key paths of values to extract. **Overrides extractAll when specified.** Paths use `/`-separated key names.

Introduced in version **M39**. These rules take precedence over the older extractAll + excludeKeys approach.

Examples:
- `/widgets/body.*` — extract all keys under `/widgets/body`
- `/messages/.*|/labels/.*` — extract from multiple subtrees

#### Include Isolated Strings
Extracts string values that appear in arrays without an associated key. For example, in `[\"Hello\", \"World\"]`, the strings have no key and would not normally be extracted.

#### Exclude Keys
Keys whose string values should be excluded from extraction (when extractAll is true) or included (when extractAll is false — inverted behavior).

When `useFullPath` is enabled, regex patterns apply to full paths like `^.*?/excludedStructure/.*`.

#### Sub-filter Rules
Specify an Okapi filter ID (e.g. `okf_html`) to process the content of all translatable text values with a sub-filter.

> **Note:** Cannot be used together with inline codes. If sub-filtering is enabled, inline code detection is ignored.

### Keys

#### Use as Name
Uses the JSON key as the `resname` (resource name) for the extracted text unit.

#### Use Full Path
Uses the complete path from root to the key (e.g. `/menu/popup/menuitem/value`) instead of just the immediate key name. Requires `useAsName` to be enabled.

#### Leading Slash
Controls whether the full key path starts with a `/` character. Only applies when `useFullPath` is enabled.

#### Use ID Stack
Builds text unit IDs by stacking nested JSON keys.

#### ID Rules
Regex matching keys whose **values** become the `resname` (Block ID) of sibling text units. Overrides `useAsName`.

Introduced in version **M39**.

Example: Given `{\"key\": \"datePicker_march\", \"text\": \"March\"}`, setting idRules to `key` produces `resname=\"datePicker_march\"` for the \"March\" text unit.

### Metadata

#### Note Rules
Regex matching keys whose values are attached as translator notes (`<note>` in XLIFF) to sibling text units.

Introduced in version **M39**.

#### Meta Rules
Regex matching keys whose values are added as generic metadata (`<context-group>` in XLIFF) to sibling text units.

Introduced in version **M39**.

#### Max Width Rules
Regex matching keys whose numeric values define maximum width constraints for sibling text units. Only one matching key is allowed per hierarchy level. For nested arrays, each level can have its own maxwidth definition.

Introduced in version **M39**.

### Max Width Size Unit
The string value used as the `size-unit` attribute on XLIFF elements when maxwidthRules extracts length restrictions. Only meaningful when maxwidthRules is configured.

### Output

#### Escape Slashes
Controls whether forward slashes (`/`) are escaped as `\\/` in the output JSON.

### Inline Codes

#### Enabled
Enables pattern-based detection of inline codes within extracted text. Cannot be used together with sub-filtering.

#### Rules
Regex patterns that identify inline codes. Default patterns detect printf-style specifiers (`%s`, `%d`), escape sequences (`\\n`, `\\t`), and ICU placeholders (`{0}`).

#### Merge Adjacent
Merges consecutive inline codes into a single placeholder.

#### Move Boundary Codes
Moves inline codes at segment boundaries to the skeleton (non-translatable).

## Limitations

- Comments within a JSON string are parsed as part of the string content, not as comments. A configured sub-filter will then process these as true comments.
- Inline codes and sub-filtering cannot be used simultaneously.

## Notes

- JSON files are normally in Unicode encoding, but the filter supports any encoding with BOM detection, charset declaration, or default encoding fallback.
- Line-break style of the output matches the input document.
- Though not technically legal in JSON, the filter supports `//`, `#`, `/* */`, and `<!-- -->` comments.
- The M39+ extraction rules (pathRules, noteRules, idRules, metaRules) override the corresponding older rules when specified.

## Examples

### Basic Extraction
```json
{\"menu\": {
  \"value\": \"File\",
  \"popup\": {
    \"menuitem\": [
      {\"value\": \"New\"},
      {\"value\": \"Open\"},
      {\"value\": \"Close\"}
    ]
  }
}}
```
All string values with keys are extracted by default.

### ID Rules with Sibling Keys
Input:
```json
[
  {\"key\": \"datePicker_marchMonth\", \"text\": \"March\"},
  {\"key\": \"datePicker_aprilMonth\", \"text\": \"April\"}
]
```
With `idRules: \"key\"`, produces text units with `resname=\"datePicker_marchMonth\"` and `resname=\"datePicker_aprilMonth\"`.
